title: Sprint Burndown Chart (Ideal vs Actual)
description: An interactive Chart.js sprint burndown showing the ideal linear burndown vs a real-world actual burndown — including a Day 3-4 plateau (blocker), a Day 5 breakthrough, a Day 7 scope-creep tick upward, and a missed sprint goal at Day 10. A second chart shows team velocity over the last six sprints with a trendline, and a toggle overlays a velocity-based forecast for the next sprint.
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Information Systems
Project Management
Agile
Scrum
learningObjectives: Read a burndown chart and distinguish ideal from actual progress
Identify scope-change events, blockers, and breakthroughs from the chart's shape
Calculate average team velocity from sprint history
Forecast a realistic next-sprint commitment using velocity rather than aspiration
prerequisites: Basic familiarity with Scrum sprints and story points

controls.description: Hover data points to see what happened that day

assumptions: Velocity history of six prior sprints used for forecasting
limitations: Not draggable — users cannot edit actual data points
Single fixed scenario; forecast is a simple linear projection
